evidence_quotes.quote: forced online video game stores, including Steam and Itch.io, to remove adult games from their storefronts due to their policies despite these otherwise being legal to sell

paragraphs.text: I believe this act needs to include regulation targeting payment processors in response to the recent and long ongoing censorship of adult content performed by them. This was most recently brought to my attention through the recent events where payment processors including Visa and MasterCard and Stripe forced online video game stores, including Steam and Itch.io, to remove adult games from their storefronts due to their policies despite these otherwise being legal to sell. These actions disproportionately affect LBTQI+ developers and players and represent a massive step backwards in terms of freedom of expression. I strongly believe payment processors should not be allowed to refuse to or process or hinder the processing of payments for legal transacations. Furthermore, this act should include regulation forwarding the goals of the Stop Killing Games citizens initiative (https://eci.ec.europa.eu/045/public/).
